title: Module 1
content:
Strategic management-Definition and scope, evolution, significance of
strategic management in contemporary business environments. Crafting a
vision statement, setting SMART objectives , aligning vision and objectives
with organizational goals. Schools of thought in strategic management –
classical, evolutionary, processual, systematic perspective. Strategy- contents,
process, practices. Understanding strategic fit, types. Configuration
perspective. Core competence- define, identify , link to competitive
advantage.

title: Module 2
content:
Sources of sustained competitive advantage- cost leadership and
differentiation strategies – innovation and technology –branding and
reputation management. Business processes – role in value creation, and
capabilities based strategy , integrating business process with strategic
decision making. Michael Porter’s five forces framework. Diversification –
motives, rationale, risks and benefits. Related and unrelated diversification

title: Module 3
content:
Understanding Strategic groups within Industries, exploring  the stages of
industry life cycle and their implications for strategy formulation. Generic
strategies – Introduction , characteristics, advantages, selection and
implementation. Generic strategies and the value chain- mapping , aligning.
Diversification – motives, rationale, risks and benefits. Related and unrelated
diversification

title: Module 4
content:
Business portfolio analysis models, tools, implications of portfolio
management decisions. Expansion strategies –organic vs inorganic growth,
Integration strategies – vertical, horizontal, conglomerate , Diversification
strategies – concentric, horizontal, conglomerate. Strategic alliances, joint
ventures, mergers and acquisitions. The 7s framework – Introduction,
interrelationships among the 7s elements, assessing organizational alignment.
Strategic control – define, importance, types. Corporate governance-
principles and practices.
